title: Nested zones, boundaries, and rendering quality
description: Complete nested zone/boundary rendering and dense-diagram quality work: zone hierarchy display, boundary nesting, overlay conflict priority, badge collapse/inspector handoff, edge/label legibility hardening, and acceptance examples.
triage_notes: IN PROGRESS. Cut from product principles rendering quality plus 03 zone view/nesting and overlay readability sections. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: fixed observed comprehensive-sample label overlap where zone labels (e.g. OnPrem/On-premises) collided with member nodes by reserving 36px top header padding for zone/boundary boxes and widening lane gap to keep adjacent boxes separated. Added layout regression test for zone label header clearance. Actual comprehensive sample OnPrem headerGap now 36px. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: added connection-density-aware component sizing in render layout. Node size now grows from graph edge degree plus permission overlay relationships so high-degree hubs get more internal padding and more port distribution room while low-degree nodes stay compact. Actual comprehensive sample CloudRun is now rendered as the largest hub size while GKECluster/Worker remain compact. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: changed permission overlay rendering from center-to-center straight lines to side-aware orthogonal paths with distributed source/target ports and stacked target-side labels. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: dense permission overlays now collapse long role labels and role badges into short in-node permission summaries; actual comprehensive sample permission overlay has 0 rendered roles/... texts and 9 permission summaries. Completion: nested modeled zones/boundaries render legibly; dense diagrams remain inspectable; no edge-over-node/shared-endpoint regressions in tests/examples. Remaining: nested zone hierarchy display, boundary nesting quality, selected highlight, diagnostics-to-diagram focus, broader non-permission edge-label collision handling.
